title: Boolean NOT operator ! does not work on strings
notes: In LSL, the ! operator is integer-only. Applying it to a string causes a type mismatch compile error. To test for an empty string, compare explicitly with "". Likewise, testing a key for NULL_KEY requires (my_key == NULL_KEY), not !my_key.

title: Cached owner key not updated after ownership transfer
notes: When an object is transferred to a new resident, any cached key owner variable retains the previous owner UUID. The standard fix is to call llResetScript() when CHANGED_OWNER is detected in the changed event; state_entry then runs fresh and assigns the correct new owner via llGetOwner(). Scripts that skip this pattern will use a stale owner key for permission checks, data lookups, or any owner-gated logic.
